title: SNDL Reports Full Year and Fourth Quarter 2023 Financial and Operational Results
text: SNDL achieved record performance metrics in 2023. The company reported net revenue of $909.0 million, representing a 28% increase from $712.2 million in 2022. Gross profit reached $190.4 million (21% of sales), up 36% year-over-year. The company noted significant cash flow improvements. Cash flow was negative $84.5 million in 2023, compared to negative $278.7 million, a 70% year-over-year improvement. Notably, SNDL achieved positive cash flow in the second half of 2023, totaling $9.6 million, with positive free cash flow of $17.7 million during the same period. CEO Zach George emphasized the transformational nature of 2023: This period was foundational as we acquired Valens, welcomed top-tier leaders and streamlined our balance sheet, positioning SNDL for improved performance in 2024 and beyond. Key operational developments included: Acquisition of The Valens Company, Closure of the Olds, Alberta cultivation facility, Portfolio optimization reducing SKUs from 327 to 125, Monetization of 35 million grams of excess biomass. Liquor Retail: $578.9 million revenue (25% increase), with 170 locations across three banners. Cannabis Retail: Record $290.0 million revenue (41% increase), with 187 retail locations. Cannabis Operations: $87.1 million revenue (96% increase), though experiencing negative gross margins. SNDL maintains robust financial positioning with $766.7 million of unrestricted cash, marketable securities and investments and no outstanding debt.
